title: The CIA’s Mind Control Program (Ft. Tom O’Neill)
summary: Tom O'Neill discusses his 20-year investigation into the Manson murders, revealing connections between Charles Manson and the CIA's MK Ultra mind control program. He presents evidence that Manson may have been an unwitting subject of government brainwashing experiments, and that the murders may have been connected to Operation Chaos and COINTELPRO efforts to discredit the counterculture movement.

acronyms.definition: A CIA brainwashing program developed from the late 1940s through early 1950s, originally defensive against Soviet/Chinese techniques but evolved into offensive program aimed at creating programmed assassins, controlling memory, and mass conversion using drugs, hypnosis, and sensory deprivation
FBI program designed to neutralize the left-wing movement and black militant movement in the United States, infiltrating groups and provoking them to commit crimes
Legal mechanism for requesting government documents
A response to FOIA requests where the CIA neither confirms nor denies any relationship, which the guest suggests indicates CIA affiliation

events.description: Jolly West reported to the CIA that he had developed technology to implant false memories and remove true memories using drugs and hypnosis
Operation Chaos began in San Francisco to neutralize left-wing and black militant movements
Manson arrived in San Francisco and began forming his cult at the Haight-Ashbury
Two Black US Organization members were killed at UCLA campus, which the FBI later admitted was orchestrated by FBI agents
Sharon Tate and four others were murdered at the Tate house on August 8th (Tate-LaBianca murders)
John Marks, a State Department official, found MK Ultra financial files and brought them to Congress
MK Ultra program was exposed by a whistleblower
Tom O'Neill received a magazine assignment to write about the 30th anniversary of the Tate-LaBianca murders

key_claims.claim: Jolly West reported to the CIA in 1955 that he had developed technology to implant false memories and remove true memories using drugs and hypnosis without a person's knowledge
The CIA censored West's 1955 report before submitting it to Congress, removing the paragraph about successful memory manipulation
Jack Ruby had a psychotic break within 48 hours of Jolly West's private meeting with him, after which he could never string three coherent sentences together
Reeve Whitson told close associates before his death that he was working undercover infiltrating the Manson family for a government agency and was at the crime scene the night of the murders
MK Ultra research was being conducted at all the federal institutions where Manson was held from age 12 or 13
Prosecutor Vince Bugliosi erased evidence of suspicious intelligence-connected figures from the Manson prosecution
Roger Smith, Manson's parole officer, specialized in amphetamines, LSD, and their effects on adolescent gang members who become violent
Whitey Bulger and Ted Kaczynski were both subjects of MK Ultra experiments
The Spahn Ranch was raided and bugged by authorities before the Tate murders, potentially allowing them to hear Manson plotting
